pages.raw_text:
The Civic league has entered into
the job of ousting Commissioner
ONO and this afternoon every sign
is that before the day is over
Mayor lAoore will have secured a
successful frameup of the council
whereby he can secure the six
votes necessary OF eliminate ONO.
A special meeting for that purpose
will probably be called for tonight.


Several caucuses of republicans
were held this morning The first
was on the street corner between
Jake Schiller and e. V. Lambert
of the Third ward. and r. l.. Dalke
of the Fourth. 1t was agreed be-
tween them that If Joe Phillips,
of the Fifth, who has been stead
fast friend to ONO, was agreeable
they would vote for the mayors
appointee. But IL was necessary
to secure the aid Of the Civic
league to fix Phillips mind, 1t Is
said.


The four then got after Capt.
John Gray Of the Second ward, and
the latest report was that the cap.
tain, under the pressure brought to
bear. was weakening. All of those
five were staunch friends to ONO
and have practically agreed among
themselves to allow him to go.


1t is now up to the mayor to pro-
vide suitable man for Omo's
shoes. And where does he go? No
where but to the Washington
Water Power Co. and secured God.
frey Murphey, chief foreman Of
construction with the company. 1t
has always been limited that the
w. w. p. Co. could get anything
It wanted around the city hall from
the mayor or any Of the rest. If
Murphey takes Omo's place 1t will
still have more Of cinch.


John Wetzel and the mayor have
kissed and made up, figuratively,
after the breach that occurred. and
along with Murphey's name, when
the frameup Is complete. Wetzel's
will be submitted again for Roy s
place instead of Omo's, as pre-
yiously,
